title: Differentiation of Human iPSCs into Microglia-like Cells
author: Jae-Hyeon Park

steps.title: Maintain iPSCs on Matrigel-coated plates in E8 medium
steps.instruction: Maintain iPSCs on Matrigel-coated plates in E8 medium.

steps.title: Ensure iPSCs are healthy and ~70–80% confluent before starting differentiation
steps.instruction: Ensure iPSCs are healthy and ~70–80% confluent before starting differentiation.

steps.title: Avoid cultures with high spontaneous differentiation
steps.instruction: Avoid cultures with high spontaneous differentiation.

steps.title: Warm all media and reagents to...
steps.instruction: Warm all media and reagents to room temperature or 37°C as appropriate.

steps.title: Dissociate iPSCs into single cells (Day
steps.instruction: Dissociate iPSCs into single cells (Day

steps.title: Aspirate iPSC culture medium
steps.instruction: Aspirate iPSC culture medium.

steps.title: Wash cells 1× with PBS
steps.instruction: Wash cells 1× with PBS.

steps.title: Add TrypLE Express and incubate at 37°C until cells detach
steps.instruction: Add TrypLE Express and incubate at 37°C until cells detach.

steps.title: Gently triturate to obtain a single-cell suspension
steps.instruction: Gently triturate to obtain a single-cell suspension. 00:10:00, 5 to 10 minutes ‱ Avoid harsh pipetting to preserve viability.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.instruction: Step 2: Plate iPSCs for EB formation (Day 0) Count cells. Plate cells in ultra-low attachment 96-well plates in 100 µL Embryoid Body Medium (EBM) per well. Return plate to incubator. 00:15:00 ‱Expected result: EBs should be visible within 24 h.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.instruction: Step 3: Day 1 media change (remove ROCK inhibitor) The following day, remove 80 µL medium from each well. Replace with 100 µL fresh EBM without ROCK inhibitor. 00:20:00 ‱ Pipette slowly at the edge of the well to avoid aspirating EBs.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.instruction: Step 4: Day 2–3 daily media changes Continue culturing EBs for 2 additional days. Perform daily full media changes using fresh EBM without ROCK inhibitor. 00:20:00, per day ‱ Expected result: EBs remain compact and increase slightly in size.

steps.title: Step 5
steps.instruction: Step 5: Transfer EBs into hematopoietic culture (Day 4) Collect EBs from the 96-well plate using wide-bore tips. Transfer EBs into a conical tube and allow them to settle by gravity. Carefully remove supernatant. Transfer approximately ~200 EBs into a T75 flask containing 20 mL Hematopoietic Medium. Return flask to incubator. 00:30:00 ‱ This culture will produce primitive macrophage precursors / microglia progenitors.

steps.title: Step 6
steps.instruction: Step 6: Feed hematopoietic cultures Replace hematopoietic medium every 2 days with fresh HM. 00:15:00, per feeding ‱ Expected result: floating progenitor-like cells begin to emerge over time.

steps.title: Step 7
steps.instruction: Step 7: Monitor emergence of microglia progenitor cells (MPCs) Observe cultures starting Day 10. Microglia progenitor cells (MPCs) typically appear between Day 12–18. ‱ Variable‱ Expected result: large, round, refractive floating cells in the supernatant.

steps.title: Step 8
steps.instruction: Step 8: Collect microglia progenitors Collect culture supernatant containing floating MPCs. Transfer to conical tubes. Centrifuge at 250 × g for 5 min. Aspirate supernatant. Resuspend pellet in Microglia Maturation Medium (MMM). 00:15:00

steps.title: Step 9
steps.instruction: Step 9: Seed progenitors for terminal differentiation Seed approximately 4 million cells per T75 flask into non-coated 75 cm² flasks. Add MMM to a final volume appropriate for flask culture (typically 15–20 mL). Return to incubator. 00:15:00 ‱ Use non-coated flasks (coating is not required).

steps.title: Step 10
steps.instruction: Step 10: Differentiate progenitors into iMGLs Culture cells in MMM for 10 days. Perform full media changes every other day. 240:00:00, 10 days total ‱Expected result: progressive microglial morphology with increased adherence and branching.

steps.title: Floating MPCs appear between Day 12–18
steps.instruction: Floating MPCs appear between Day 12–18. After 10 days in MMM, cells should display microglia-like morphology and express markers such as: CD11b CX3CR1 IBA1 P2RY12 TMEM119 Mature iMGLs can be used for: scRNA-seq flow cytometry phagocytosis assays inflammatory stimulation (IFN_γ_, LPS, etc.)

steps.title: Problem
steps.instruction: Problem: Poor EB formation Possible cause: low iPSC viability after dissociation Solution: reduce pipetting stress, use fresh TrypLE or Accutase, confirm iPSCs are healthy before starting Problem: EBs break apart Possible cause: harsh pipetting during media changes Solution: use wide-bore tips and pipette gently from the edge of wells Problem: Low progenitor (MPC) yield Possible cause: cytokines degraded or incorrect feeding schedule Solution: prepare fresh cytokine aliquots, maintain strict every-2-day feeding schedule Problem: Poor terminal differentiation Possible cause: progenitors collected too early Solution: wait until Day 12–18 window when progenitors are robust and abundant

steps.title: Embryoid Body Medium (EBM) Prepared in E8 medium
steps.instruction: Embryoid Body Medium (EBM) Prepared in E8 medium : ROCK inhibitor: 10 µM BMP-4: 50 ng/mL SCF: 20 ng/mL VEGF-121: 50 ng/mL

steps.title: Hematopoietic Medium (HM) Prepared in X-VIVO 15
steps.instruction: Hematopoietic Medium (HM) Prepared in X-VIVO 15 : GlutaMAX: 2 mM β-Mercaptoethanol: 55 µM M-CSF: 100 ng/mL IL-3: 25 ng/mL

steps.title: Microglia Maturation Medium (MMM) Prepared in Advanced RPMI
steps.instruction: Microglia Maturation Medium (MMM) Prepared in Advanced RPMI : GlutaMAX: 2 mM IL-34: 100 ng/mL GM-CSF: 10 ng/mL
